triangulated_patterns.pattern: Layer depth is the primary R² ceiling, not quantization noise
evidence: R² inversely correlates with model size: SmolLM2(0.94) > 0.8B(0.878) > 7B(0.855) > 3B(0.688)
3B has 36 layers vs 28 for 7B — 12 more compounding error points
Per-layer R² on 7B: L17=0.93, L27=-0.84 (last layer worse than zero)
Cosine similarity plateaus at 0.788 from representational saturation
triangulated_patterns.elaboration: The dominant root cause (abductive score: 0.91) is terminal-layer representational anisotropy. Each additional transformer layer provides another opportunity for next-token-prediction gradients to rotate hidden states away from velocity-informative subspaces. The 12-layer gap between 0.8B (24L) and 3B (36L) fully predicts the 0.878→0.688 drop if each layer corrupts ~0.016 R². Quantization noise (4-bit AWQ-Marlin) is real but secondary (adversarial attack severity: 0.3).

triangulated_patterns.pattern: Global per-dimension normalization conflates heterogeneous layer dynamics
evidence: Current normalization applies one v_mean/v_std across ALL layers and dimensions
Per-layer R² ranges from 0.93 to -0.84 — evidence of radically different distributions
L0 has gain 13.6× vs ~0.5 elsewhere, suggesting it's an outlier distribution
Simpson's paradox analogy: aggregation across heterogeneous strata reverses signal
triangulated_patterns.elaboration: The dialectical lens ranks this as the #1 impediment (0.91). Per-layer normalization would allow each layer's predictor to operate in a well-conditioned subspace. The analogical lens maps this to multi-source domain adaptation where each source requires its own whitening transform. Expected R² gain: +0.08-0.12.

triangulated_patterns.pattern: NTFS I/O bottleneck is the highest-leverage operational intervention
evidence: Training runs at 0.023 ep/s — 50× below GPU potential
3 reinforcing loops (R1, R2, R3) all amplify I/O constraints
Von Neumann bottleneck analogy: data movement, not compute, is limiting
Trajectory lens: 5 sessions wasted under status quo with R² stuck at 0.72-0.74
triangulated_patterns.elaboration: Moving data from NTFS to NVMe SSD or RAM disk directly breaks the R2 and R3 reinforcing loops. Estimated 10-20× wall-clock speedup, enabling 2000+ epochs in hours instead of days. The trajectory lens predicts this costs <1 engineering day and prevents 5 wasted sessions.

triangulated_patterns.pattern: The cosine=0.788 plateau signals a representational fixed point, not a training limit
evidence: Cosine sim saturates at 0.788 while train loss continues decreasing
Last-layer R² = -0.84 on 7B — representations are actively anti-predictive
Curvature κ spikes to 3.0 at L26 (vs ~1.5 elsewhere) — phase transition signature
Markov chain attractor analogy: post-mixing-time representations have no trajectory-specific info
triangulated_patterns.elaboration: The blending lens reveals final-layer representations are not merely noisy but actively rotated away from velocity-relevant subspaces by LM gradient pressure. The 0.788 ceiling represents the cosine of the maximal rotation angle that doesn't degrade LM loss. This implies: (a) skip last 8-10 layers for velocity prediction, (b) add a velocity-auxiliary loss at intermediate layers during TT training to prevent rotation.

triangulated_patterns.pattern: Ensemble of small TTs can exploit per-layer variance decomposition
evidence: Small models (SmolLM2 d_model=512) achieve R²=0.94 individually
Per-layer R² variance (0.93 to -0.84) is exploitable through layer-wise voting
3× d_model=512 ensemble has 2× params of d_model=1280 but O(d²) attention cost
Adversarial attack (limited diversity) is valid but mitigated by different seeds+checkpoints
triangulated_patterns.elaboration: An ensemble of 3 small TTs (d_model=512 each) trains 4× faster than one d_model=1280 TT (since attention is O(d²)), and can use per-layer confidence-weighted averaging. The dialectical lens suggests learned weighting over uniform averaging. Expected R²: 0.72-0.75 from ensemble vs 0.69-0.71 from single 1280.

analogical.key_insight: The R² inverse scaling and cosine plateau follow the same structure as Markov chain stationary distributions. Models 'mix' to a fixed point beyond layer ~25 on 3B.

dialectical.key_insight: All contradictions center on depth-dependent representation drift vs a single global predictor. Pre-training ranked LAST (0.31 impediment).

blending.key_insight: Layer count confounds the apparent size-R² relationship. A 3B/24L variant would likely achieve R²≈0.87.

systems.key_insight: Compounding Degradation (R1) is the dominant reinforcing loop. Breaking it via structured prediction or per-layer goals is the highest-leverage intervention.

abductive.key_insight: Terminal-layer representational anisotropy (0.91 combined) is the best single explanation. Most falsifiable: 3B in fp16 should confirm quantization is secondary.

trajectory.key_insight: Status quo leads to R² stuck at 0.72-0.74 and 5 wasted sessions. SSD migration is the highest-leverage action.

metacognitive.key_insight: The analysis may be overconfident that R² improvement is always possible. An info-theoretic ceiling may exist and should be estimated first.

inspiration.key_insight: PCA/EOF decomposition before prediction (from climate modeling) and noise-floor regularized loss (from rate-distortion theory) are the strongest cross-domain adaptations.

adversarial.key_insight: Strongest attack: info-theoretic bound on d_model (severity 0.8). Data augmentation is the weakest proposal (severity 0.7 attack on SNR).

novel_syntheses.synthesis: Neural ODE in PCA latent space with noise-floor regularized loss
novel_syntheses.description: Apply PCA to (batch×36, 2048) trajectory matrix, keep top K=150 components (95% var). Predict velocity in PCA latent space via a Neural ODE parameterized by 2-layer MLP. Use noise-floor margin loss: L = max(0, |y-ŷ| - α·ε_f)². This addresses: (1) quantization noise via PCA denoising, (2) compounding errors via ODE smoothness regularization, (3) dimension reduction for faster training.

novel_syntheses.synthesis: Layer-gated regime-switching ensemble with confidence weighting
novel_syntheses.description: Train a learned gating network that assigns each layer to one of 3 regime experts ('structure formation' L1-10, 'compositional refinement' L11-25, 'output tuning' L26-36). Each expert is a small TT (d_model=384). Final prediction = weighted sum of expert outputs with per-layer confidence weights learned via softmax over validation per-layer R².

root_causes.what_would_disprove: PCA-whitening L27 features then regressing yields R² > 0.80

root_causes.root_cause: Compounding error across 36 layers (systems loop R1)
root_causes.intervention: Add skip-level prediction targets or residual connections in TT

root_causes.root_cause: Global normalization conflating layer dynamics (dialectical rank: 0.91)
root_causes.intervention: Per-layer z-score normalization with learnable affine transforms

leverage_points.point: Move data to SSD + memory-mapped loading
Skip last 8-10 layers for velocity prediction

trajectories.if_unchanged: R² saturates at 0.72-0.74, 5+ sessions wasted on hyperparameter exploration

trajectories.if_ssd_and_per_layer_norm: R² reaches 0.80-0.85 within 2-3 sessions, unlocks further optimization

ranked_recommendations.recommendation: Migrate data from NTFS to SSD (ext4/XFS) + memory-mapped training
Predict last 26 layers only (skip layers 27-36 which have degraded representations)
Ensemble of 3 small TTs (d_model=512) with per-layer confidence weighting
PCA/EOF spectral decomposition before prediction (2048→150 dim latent space)
Noise-floor regularized loss (margin loss with per-feature quantization thresholds)
Data augmentation: trajectory mixing + random layer omission
Multi-task learning: velocity + contrastive inter-layer loss (0.1× weight)

top3_implementation_1_per_layer_norm.title: Per-layer normalization + loss weighting
changes: In run_train_gen_tt_3b.py: compute per-layer v_mean (n_layers, d_model) and v_std (n_layers, d_model) from validation set instead of global (1, d_model)
Normalize each layer independently: (v[l,:] - v_mean[l,:]) / v_std[l,:]
Add learnable per-layer affine after normalization: γ[l] * v_norm + β[l] (add to TrajectoryTransformer)
Optionally: weight loss per layer by 1/v_std[l]² (inverse variance weighting — layers with low noise get higher weight)
Add per-layer R² monitoring in validation loop
top3_implementation_1_per_layer_norm.expected_result: R² increase from 0.688 to 0.77-0.81. Reduced L27 negative effect. More stable training.
top3_implementation_1_per_layer_norm.risks: Per-layer normalization adds 36×2048 = 74K parameters. Marginal risk: will need slightly more epochs to converge per-layer stats.

top3_implementation_2_ssd_migration.title: SSD migration + memory-mapped data loading
changes: Copy data from /mnt/windows/trajs_3b_bs64 to /home/filip/... (internal SSD) or /tmp/opencode/ for RAM-speed
Modify data loading: consolidate all batches into single .pt file via torch.cat + save with torch.save(..., _use_new_zipfile_serialization=False)
Load with torch.load(path, map_location='cpu', mmap=True) for instant random access to any trajectory
Pre-compute normalization stats once and store in norm_stats.pt
Remove the per-epoch file-permutation shuffle (inefficient): shuffle indices into consolidated tensor instead
Increase batch size to GPU-max if I/O bottleneck resolved
top3_implementation_2_ssd_migration.expected_result: Epoch time drops from ~44s to ~2-5s (10-20× speedup). 2000 epochs achievable in 2-3 hours vs 24h. Enables rapid hyperparameter exploration.
top3_implementation_2_ssd_migration.risks: SSD space: 88K × 36 × 2048 × 2 bytes = ~13GB. Should fit. Use float16 to halve if needed.

top3_implementation_3_skip_degraded_laye.title: Predict only layers 0-25 (skip last 10 layers)
changes: During data loading: slice hidden_seqs to [:n_layers_to_predict] and velocity_targets to [:n_layers_to_predict]
n_layers_to_predict = n_layers - 10 (start with 26, evaluate R² on held-out set)
Also experiment: predict ALL layers but mask loss for last 10 layers with λ=0.1 weight
Monitor: does R² on early layers improve when later layers are excluded from gradient?
top3_implementation_3_skip_degraded_laye.expected_result: R² improves from 0.688 to 0.72-0.75. Reduced error signal contamination from collapsed last layers.
top3_implementation_3_skip_degraded_laye.risks: If early layers also benefit from cross-attending to later layers (unlikely given negative R²), performance may degrade. Test with and without masking.

further_avenues: FA-1: Controlled precision experiment — collect 200 trajectories from 3B in fp16 and compare R² to 4-bit baseline. Quantifies quantization noise contribution directly.
FA-2: Neural ODE in PCA latent space — reduces 2048→150 dims, predicts continuous dynamics, natural denoising.
FA-3: Check if d_model=1280 eval matches train — verify d_model projection doesn't create eval ceiling.
FA-4: Train 3B TT using only layers 0-23 (matching 7B's depth) to test the layer-count hypothesis.
FA-5: Cross-layer contrastive loss to stabilize representations against LM gradient rotation.

recommended_next_steps.step: P0: Move trajectory data from NTFS to SSD + memory-map
P0: Implement per-layer normalization and loss weighting
P0: Add per-layer R² monitoring to training loop
P1: Skip last 8-10 degraded layers during prediction
P1: Run controlled fp16 vs 4-bit precision comparison
P2: Implement PCA denoising pipeline for trajectory preprocessing
P2: Train 3× d_model=512 ensemble with layer-wise confidence gating
P3: Multi-task with contrastive inter-layer loss
